item.title: Commerzbank held talks with UniCredit over potential deal
item.description: German bank hits back after Italian lender accused it of failing to engage

item.title: IMF warns of emerging markets’ exposure to ‘flighty’ hedge funds
item.description: Non-bank lenders rapidly reduce holdings of EM debt during shocks such as the Iran war, analysis suggests

item.title: Hedge funds make record bets against European stocks
item.description: Short positions rise sharply as traders eye economic fallout from Iran war

item.title: BP chair faces re-election battle after board blocks climate resolution
item.description: Glass Lewis recommends voting against Albert Manifold at annual meeting, while L&G also plans to oppose him

item.title: Abu Dhabi’s Mubadala Capital raises almost $1bn for latest Brazil fund
item.description: Commitment eases concerns that Middle East war will cause Gulf states to review foreign investments

item.title: Kanye West banned from entering UK after antisemitism dispute
item.description: US rapper had been due to perform at Wireless Festival in London in July

item.title: Blackstone and Tinicum agree £1.4bn deal for UK aerospace supplier Senior
item.description: Sale would mark latest takeover of a London-listed industrial group

item.title: Lessons for the AI revolutionaries from Francis Bacon
item.description: His project of mastering nature contains a warning for the excesses of our technological age

item.title: The Iran war shock is about half the size of Covid-19
item.description: An assessment almost six weeks into the latest global economic crisis

item.title: China and Europe launch rare joint space mission
item.description: Solar-focused project seeks to understand geomagnetic storms that can disrupt terrestrial communications

item.title: How the Iran war will reshape our energy future
item.description: Fatih Birol, head of the International Energy Agency, has six predictions

item.title: FirstFT: Bill Ackman’s Pershing launches bid for Universal Music
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Citigroup seeks to jump-start its money management business and Artemis II circles the Moon

item.title: Italian government poised to replace Leonardo CEO who oversaw surge in orders
item.description: Move by Italian government comes as Roberto Cingolani’s three-year term at defence group expires next month

item.title: Citi sets aggressive targets for bankers in wealth management unit
item.description: Revised goals involve net revenue and value of assets clients entrust to Citi as business has lagged Wall Street peers

item.title: Doctors’ demands could cost NHS up to £30bn a year, says Streeting
item.description: Health secretary warns that acceding to further pay rise would lead to wage claims from other staff, as latest six-day strike begins

item.title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square offers to buy Universal Music Group
item.description: Deal would shift the listing of the world’s largest music company from Amsterdam to New York

item.title: Don’t forget the Mag 7
item.description: Still a third of the S&P 500 — and wobbling a bit

item.title: SpaceX bankers bend over backwards to bag IPO role
item.description: To infinity back-scratching and beyond

item.title: Private equity buyouts nosedive
item.description: Plus, government bond demand booms, asset manager consolidation wave and Vincent in Brixton at the Orange Tree

item.title: Air India chief quits early after turbulent tenure
item.description: Campbell Wilson’s ambition to turn carrier into world-class airline marred by fatal crash of Boeing Dreamliner jet

item.title: Jamie Dimon angers private credit rivals . . . again
item.description: The JPMorgan chief warns that private credit losses will be larger than many expect

item.title: Top record labels and start-up Suno hit impasse in AI-generated music talks
item.description: One executive says there is ‘no path’ towards licensing deal under current proposal

item.title: Jeff Bezos’s new lab hires xAI co-founder from OpenAI
item.description: Kyle Kosic joins Project Prometheus, the secretive start-up working on systems that can understand the physical world

item.title: The internet is deciding what to forget
item.description: Do digital bananas in Hawaiian shirts chatting up pineapples need to be saved for posterity? Probably not

item.title: Brussels banking rules could hit lenders and UK-EU ‘reset’, City figures warn
item.description: Concern that efforts to boost European defence spending could also be damaged

item.title: The future of global trade won’t depend on the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A new infrastructure is being built rapidly to avoid the strategic chokepoint and ensure global energy and food security

item.title: Europe’s fossil fuel dependence poses risks to price stability
item.description: Repeated cost shocks make a transition to cleaner energy critical

item.title: US counts cost of equipment destroyed in Iran war
item.description: Lost aircraft and radars contribute to estimated $500mn daily price tag and leave US exposed elsewhere

item.title: UK pubs and restaurants face ‘perma-crisis’ after energy shock
item.description: Hospitality sector already hit by increases to business rates and wages

item.title: JPMorgan to build Canary Wharf’s tallest tower after City airport approval
item.description: Bank wins clearance for 265-metre skyscraper after talks over low-flying aircraft
